title: Artificial allosteric protein switches with machine-learning-designed receptors
research_objectives: Engineer artificial allosteric protein systems with programmable inputs and outputs
Develop machine-learning-designed minimal ligand-binding domains as biosensor receptors
Demonstrate that global conformational change is not required for allosteric regulation
Create single-component allosteric switches with diverse output types
Engineer logic gates (YES and AND) for multi-input biosensing
Validate functional utility in cellular and bioelectronic applications

research_scope.focus_area: Protein allostery and synthetic allosteric protein engineering
research_scope.methodology: Machine learning design combined with protein engineering and synthetic biology

allosteric_regulation_principles.biological_importance: Underlies most information and energy processing in biology
allosteric_regulation_principles.synthetic_biology_objective: Central goal to develop artificial systems for biotechnology
allosteric_regulation_principles.key_insight: Allostery possible without global conformational change through conformational entropy

design_challenge_addressed.traditional_limitation: Paucity of natural domains undergoing global conformation change upon ligand binding
design_challenge_addressed.assumption_challenged: Global conformational change presumed necessary for allosteric output control
design_challenge_addressed.new_paradigm: Conformational entropy changes in internal dynamics can regulate allostery

machine_learning_design_approach.goal: Generate minimal ligand-binding domains for biosensor receptors
machine_learning_design_approach.advantage: Enables conversion of domains without global conformational change

receptor_domain.characteristics: Minimal ligand-binding domain engineered by ML
receptor_domain.ligand_specificity: Tunable for diverse small molecules, peptides, proteins
receptor_domain.conformational_behavior: No requirement for global conformation change

reporter_domain.function: Transduces ligand binding into measurable output

linker_design.principle: Locate linker away from conformational flexibility regions
